description: Food enters and speech leaves through 'phulae', the opening and cavity also used for drinking and breathing. 'lathoe' (tongue) and 'kulai' (tooth) name parts within it. Voice and speech remain actions or sounds rather than senses of the body-part noun.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close without sealing them and let unvoiced air pass between them for 'ph', then raise the back of the tongue into high-back rounded 'u'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and open into stressed central unrounded 'a'. Move the tongue forward into middle-height unrounded 'e' for the separate final syllable.
sound_symbolism: The word begins at the lips with 'ph', then moves the tongue through a lateral contact before finishing in an open vowel. The mouth performs much of the form it describes, which makes the sequence easy to remember.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The mouth makes speech bodily action: intention matters, but so do the words and tone that actually leave it.

semantic_domains.communication: the body opening through which spoken language is articulated
semantic_domains.physical: the opening and cavity used for eating, drinking, breathing, and speech
